granularity: column — NO pixel ground truth exists for this target; the GT is the published papyrological reading at column level (22 columns; Coll. 1-4 traces, 5-22 text) by eight papyrologists' consensus

geometry.render_recipe: vesuvius-autoresearch repro/sota_data/render_cli.py --tifxyz <above> --volume <above> --region Y X SIZE --level 2 (renderer gate-validated on this scroll: clean-triple NCC 0.7799 vs released w011 surface volume, pre-registered gate 0.60)

prediction_contract: probability map at grid resolution over the full grid or a rectangular sub-extent (pass --origin Y X); only columns and gutters fully inside the extent are scored and they are listed in the scorecard
license: column coordinates + transcription facts derive from Angelotti et al., 'Complete virtual unwrapping and reading of a rolled Herculaneum papyrus' (scrollprize.org/pdf/main.pdf), CC BY-NC 4.0 — attribution required, non-commercial. ScrollGT code is MIT; this data directory inherits CC BY-NC.

registration.method: preprint page-3 figure strips (ink reading + labeled col brackets) shape-registered onto the merged tifxyz valid mask; scale 4.7 grid px per figure px + dy 19 recovered independently by all 3 strips; strip tiling closure 3 px over the 30097-px grid; exactly 22 bracket intervals
registration.flagged: cols 9 and 16 span strip-crop gaps (bbox edges +/-250 grid px); gutters adjacent to them are excluded by the scorer

role: held-out at column level for every model in baselines/ (no distilled arm trained on any PHerc-1667 data; the core team's own pipeline obviously trained on this scroll — external submissions must disclose 1667 exposure)
honesty_notes: column-level scores measure CONSISTENCY WITH THE READING (signal in text columns vs gutters, line periodicity), not letter accuracy. A high col_gutter_auc is necessary-not-sufficient evidence of reading.
